## [00:00 - 03:00]

So in this example, I want to show you a different style of working on the AOV. This one in particular is for my own product. So this is a CBO cookbook. And when we launched this, we launched it at a lower price, but we wanted to figure out how we could entice people to add additional items to their purchase that were complimentary and also allowing us to increase the AOV so that with our CPAs, when we started running out, we could then work on something which was easier for us to scale up. So in this example here, we created a product and in the cart itself. So this site was based on click panels where we were able to add a simple upsell. So by this point, we've added, we've got the customer interested in the product. So one of the important things about this is the placement of the cross-sell. So this is a cross-sell. Once you've got them down to this, so like, I see some pages where they put the additional sell kind of further up before they've collected the payment details. The problem with that is that person hasn't yet committed. So I want to offer it at a time where I don't want to impact the conversion rate. So for example, if they get to this stage, they put their card number in, if they don't want this, absolutely fine. You know, I want them to continue and purchase. But if this is something they are interested in, then it's simply a case of just clicking that link, you get that added to your cart and then you get the updates. Your purchase is updated with this as well. So a few things about this. So the core price of the product at the time was $197. The cross-sell item is from a live mastermind. The value of that is $1500, the full price, and we're offering it at $297. This is the only way that people can get this product for $297 at the time of purchasing this product. Now you might look at that design and rightly say it's not the greatest design. It's just a default from click funnels with a few color changes, but it did its job. This, combined with the following upsell on the next page, helps us to increase the AOV by around 40%. So that's an extra 40% revenue per person. So rather than, for example, generating $197 from every purchase, we were able to bump that up to a higher value simply based on offering complimentary products that were going to help the person buying this product to get even more value out of my training, for example. So when we look at increasing AOV, there's lots of different ways of doing this. We've given you, you know, being through examples for e-com, product bundling, looking at sites

## [03:00 - 04:56]

like Amazon, for example. And this is now kind of thinking more about complimentary products that's going to help the user to experience your core products even better. So, you know, when you're thinking about your product, whether you're in e-com, in info, even mobile app installs, anything like that, it's thinking about if you're trying to get more value from them, what value can it give that's relative to the product that they're going in for. Now, the other part of this is when you're building and thinking about your upsell journey and your cross-sell journey, also think about downselling as well. I'll give you an example. So, you know, one of the things that we did discuss is if they took the 197 plus the 297, great. On the next page, we have a 997 offer. If they don't take that, then maybe there's another product we offer, which is $97 or $200, or even $47, whatever it is, because you're edging up the average order value and you're increasing the value for the person coming through your funnel, because they may not be aware of all of your products. So, this, for example, is a funnel that we invested heavily in, not just in terms of ads, but also organic marketing as well. That the people coming into this, how can we serve them even better? So, if they're coming into this and buying this product, is there to solve a problem or create a new opportunity? So, you know, going back to your 5W avatar, that's what I'm thinking about here is what's the angle? What's the opportunity? What's the big idea that I'm generating here? For most of the people, it's generating stability, consistency through CBO, for example, but how can I compliment you on that? And that's where we're now thinking about additional products where we can compliment that user journey. So, that's really giving you another example of how to increase your AOV using complimentary products.
